reason: Changing /etc/gshadow permissions to 0000 is a standard STIG hardening step that does not affect system boot, networking, or authentication mechanisms beyond restricting access to the shadow file. It is unlikely to cause lockout or service disruption.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Potentially restricts processes that need read access to gshadow (e.g., user management tools); May require restart of services that cache shadow data
